item.title: 周一表情包🤌 | Meme Monday 🤌
item.description:
本·哈尔彭

发表于欢迎来到另一个Meme Monday帖子！ 今天的封面图来自上周的帖子。

低品味的幽默会被模组否决。

莱昂纳多·蒙蒂尼—— 一旦暂停，本将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，本将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，本的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 ben 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 Ben 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 ben 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Ben Halpern

Posted on Nov 7

Welcome to another Meme Monday post! Today's cover image comes from last week's thread.

DEV is an inclusive space! Humor in poor taste will be downvoted by mods.

Leonardo Montini - Jul 26

Once unsuspended, ben will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by ben will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If ben is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging ben will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: ben

item.title: 没有 JavaScript 的客户端路由 | Client-side Routing without the JavaScript
item.description:
我已经有一段时间没有写一篇关于SolidJS技术创新的文章了。 自从我们使用 Streaming SSR 在服务器上添加 Suspense 以来已经两年了。 甚至可以追溯到我们在 2019 年首次引入 Suspense 用于数据获取和并发渲染时。

虽然 React 已经引入了这些概念，但为细粒度的反应系统实现它们却是另一种野兽。 需要一点想象力和完全不同的解决方案来避免差异。

这与我们最近一直在做的探索有相似的感觉。 受React Server Components和 Island 解决方案（如Marko和Astro ）的启发，Solid 迈出了进入Partial Hydration的第一步。 固体启动

自从发布 Solid 1.0 以来，我有点不知所措。 在保留未解决的问题和尝试检查更多框以供采用之间，我肯定觉得分散得很薄。 一切都表明需要一个 SSR 元框架，我什至在 1.0 版本发布之前就开始了这项工作。

社区加紧提供帮助。 但最终，为了让测试版推出，我会成为阻碍者。 为了让事情集中在发布上，我同意了，但告诉他把时间框起来，因为第二天我需要他的帮助。 第二天，他向我展示了一个演示，他不仅添加了 Isl​​ands，重新创建了 Fresh 体验，而且还添加了客户端路由。

意外岛屿

现在演示很粗糙，但令人印象深刻。 他采用了我的一个 Hackernews 演示并重新实现了递归岛。 什么是递归岛屿.. 那是你在岛屿中投影岛屿的时候：

 function MyServerComponent ( props ) { return <> { props . data && < MyClientIsland > < MyServerComponent data = { props . data . childData } / > < /MyClientIsland > } < / > }

你为什么要这个？ 好吧，岛屿有一条规则，您不能在其中导入和使用仅服务器组件。 原因是您不希望客户端能够将状态传递给他们。 为什么？ 好吧，如果客户端可以将状态传递给它们，那么它们就需要能够更新，并且由于想法是不将此 JavaScript 发送到浏览器，因此这是行不通的。 幸运的是props.children执行了这个边界。 他怎么能在这么短的时间内完成这个演示？ 嗯，这是偶然的。 它们看起来像这样：

 <div data-hk= "0-0-1-0-2" />

每个模板增加一个计数，每个嵌套组件增加一个数字。 这对于我们的单程补水至关重要。 毕竟 JSX 可以按任何顺序创建，并且可以随时解决 Suspense 边界。

但是在给定的深度，所有 id 都将按相同的顺序分配给客户端或服务器。

 function Component () { const anotherDiv = < div data - hk = " 1 " /> return < div data - hk = " 2 " > { anotherDiv } < /div > } // output < div data - hk = " 2 " > < div data - hk = " 1 " /> < /div >

此外，我添加了一个<NoHydration>组件来抑制这些 ID，以便我们可以跳过头部中的链接和样式表等水合资产。 只在服务器上运行而无需在浏览器中运行的东西。

同样不相关的是，在与 Astro 进行 Solid 集成时，我添加了一种机制来为水合根设置前缀，以防止这些 ID 重复用于不相关的岛屿。

我从来没有想过我们可以将自己的 ID 作为前缀输入。 而且由于它只是附加在末尾，我们可以从页面上的任何位置开始对服务器渲染的 Solid 页面进行水合。 使用<NoHydration>我们可以在任何时候停止 hydrating 以将孩子隔离为仅限服务器。

混合路由

对于 Islands 和 Partial Hydration 的所有好处，要不提供所有 JavaScript，您不需要在浏览器中使用该代码。 当您需要客户端呈现页面时，您需要所有代码来呈现下一页。

虽然像Turbo这样的技术已被用于在不完全重新加载页面的情况下获取和替换 HTML，但人们注意到这常常让人感到笨拙。

但不久前我们有一个想法，我们可以采用嵌套路由并仅替换 HTML 部分。 早在 3 月，Ryan Turnquist（Solid Router 的联合创始人）就制作了这个演示。 虽然没有太多的可视化演示，但它证明了我们只需 1.3kb 的 JavaScript 就可以拥有这种功能。

诀窍在于，通过点击事件的事件委托，我们可以触发客户端路由器而不需要对页面进行水合。 从那里我们可以使用 AJAX 请求下一页并传递上一页，服务器将从路由定义中确切知道它需要呈现页面的哪些嵌套部分。 使用返回的 HTML，客户端路由器可以交换内容。

完成图片

最初的演示很粗糙，但它显示了很多希望。 它仍然存在仅服务器内容的双重数据问题，这是我们需要在核心中解决的问题。 因此，我们添加了对何时在页面的仅服务器部分下创建实体资源的检测。 我们知道，如果触发数据获取的操作只能发生在服务器上，则无需将其全部序列化。 岛屿已经序列化了他们传入的道具。

我们还借此机会创建了一种机制，通过hydrate调用传递反应式上下文，允许上下文在由服务器内容分隔的岛屿之间的浏览器中工作。

有了这些，我们就为递归 Hackernews 评论演示做好了准备：

是时候进行另一种 Island 的 Hackernews 演示了。

但是我们缺少一件事。 交换 HTML 对新导航很有好处，但是当您需要刷新页面的一部分时呢？ 您不想失去客户端状态、输入焦点等... Nikhil 管理了一个可以做到这一点的版本。 但最终我们最终使用了由 Nate Moore（ Astro的）编写的 light DOM diff微变形。

有了这个，我们将 Taste 电影应用演示移植到了 13kb 的 JS 荣耀中。 搜索页面特别展示了在不丢失客户端状态的情况下重新加载。 当您键入时，即使需要更新整个嵌套面板，输入也不会失去焦点。

实体电影演示
在Github上

只是为了让您了解这是多么荒谬的小。 这是在两个电影列表页面之间导航的总 JavaScript，然后使用来自https://tastejs.com/movies/的客户端路由导航到各种框架中的电影。

注意：只有 Solid 演示使用了服务器渲染的部分，所以这有点不平等。 但重点是强调大小的差异。 其他框架正在开发类似的解决方案，例如Next中的 RSC 和Qwik中的 Containers，但这些是今天可用的演示。

结论

我们以这种方式构建的应用程序越多，我对这项技术就越兴奋。 它在各个方面都感觉像是一个单页应用程序，但它要小得多。 老实说，每次打开网络标签时，我都会感到惊讶。

我们仍在努力将其从实验中移出并巩固 API。 在服务器渲染方面还有更多的优化空间，但我们认为这里有一种新型架构的所有要素。 这很酷。



原文:

It's been a while since I wrote a piece about a SolidJS technology innovation. It's been two years now since we added Suspense on the server with Streaming SSR. And even longer to go back to when we first introduced Suspense for data fetching and concurrent rendering back in 2019.

While React had introduced these concepts, implementing them for a fine-grained reactive system was a whole other sort of beast. Requiring a little imagination and completely different solutions that avoided diffing.

And that is a similar feeling to the exploration we've been doing recently. Inspired equal parts from React Server Components and Island solutions like Marko and Astro, Solid has made it's first steps into Partial Hydration. (comparison at the bottom)

SolidStart

Since releasing Solid 1.0 I've been kinda swamped. Between keeping open issues down and trying to check off more boxes for adoption I definitely have felt spread thin. Everything pointed to the need for a SSR meta-framework, an effort I started even before the 1.0 release.

The community stepped up to help. But ultimately, for getting the beta out the door I would become the blocker. And Nikhil Saraf, never one to sit still, having recently been introduced to Fresh wanted to see if he couldn't just add Islands to SolidStart.

Wanting to keep things focused on a release, I agreed but told him to time-box it as I'd need his help the next day. The next day he showed me a demo where he did not only add Islands, recreating the Fresh experience, but he had added client-side routing.

Accidental Islands

Now the demo was rough, but it was impressive. He'd taken one of my Hackernews demos and re-implemented the recursive Islands. What are recursive Islands.. that's when you project Islands in Islands:

function MyServerComponent(props) { return <>{ props.data && <MyClientIsland> <MyServerComponent data={props.data.childData} />
 </MyClientIsland>
 }</>
}

Why would you want this? Well, there is a rule with Islands that you cannot import and use Server only components in them. The reason is you don't want the client to be able to pass state to them. Why? Well if the client could pass state to them then they'd need to be able to update and since the idea is to not send this JavaScript to the browser this wouldn't work. Luckily props.children enforces this boundary pretty well. (Assuming you disallow passing render functions/render props across Island boundaries).

function MyClientIsland() { const [state, setState] = createSignal(); // can't pass props to the children return <div>{props.children}</div>
}

How was he able to make this demo in such short order? Well, it was by chance. Solid's hydration works off of matching hierarchical IDs to templates instantiated in the DOM. They look something like this:

<div data-hk="0-0-1-0-2" />

Each template increments a count and each nested component adds another digit. This is essential for our single-pass hydration. After all JSX can be created in any order and Suspense boundaries resolved at any time.

But at a given depth all ids will be assigned in the same order client or server.

function Component() { const anotherDiv = <div data-hk="1" /> return <div data-hk="2">{anotherDiv}</div>
} // output
<div data-hk="2"> <div data-hk="1" />
</div>

Additionally, I had added a <NoHydration> component to suppress these IDs so that we could skip hydrating assets like links and stylesheets in the head. Things that only ran on the server and didn't need to run in the browser.

And also unrelated, working on the Solid integration with Astro, I had added a mechanism to set a prefix for hydration roots to prevent the duplication of these IDs for unrelated islands.

It just never occurred to me that we could feed our own IDs in as the prefix. And since it would just append on the end we could hydrate a Server rendered Solid page starting at any point on the page. With <NoHydration> we could stop hydrating at any point to isolate the children as server-only.

Hybrid Routing

For all the benefits of Islands and Partial Hydration, to not ship all the JavaScript, you need to not require that code in the browser. The moment you need to client render pages you need all the code to render the next page.

While Technologies like Turbo have been used to fetch and replace the HTML without fully reloading the page, people have noted this often felt clunky.

But we had an idea a while back that we could take our nested routing and only replace HTML partials. Back in March, Ryan Turnquist(co-creator of Solid Router) made this demo. While not much of a visual demo it proved we could have this sort of functionality with only 1.3kb of JavaScript.

The trick was that through event delegation of click events we could trigger a client router without hydrating the page. From there we could use AJAX to request the next page and pass along the previous page and the server would know from the route definition exactly what nested parts of the page it needed to render. With the returned HTML the client-side router could swap in the content.

Completing the Picture

The original demo was rough, but it showed a lot of promise. It was still had the double data problem for server-only content and this was something we needed to address in the core. So we added detection for when a Solid Resource was created under a server-only portion of the page. We knew that if what would trigger the data fetching could only happen on the server there was no need to serialize it all. Islands already serialized their props passed in.

We also took this opportunity to create a mechanism to pass reactive context through hydrate calls allowing Context to work in the browser between Islands seperated by server content.

With those in place, we were ready for the recursive Hackernews comments demo:

Time for a different sort of Island's Hackernews Demo.

🚀@solid_js + TypeScript + JSX


⚡️Built with SolidStart on @vite_js🏝️Recursive Islands🚫No duplicated data serialization😎Server Nested Routing without Reload🔥~5kb JS Payload

solid-hn-islands.netlify.app

But there was one thing we were missing. Swapping HTML was all good for new navigations but what about when you need to refresh part of the page? You wouldn't want to lose client state, input focus etc... Nikhil managed a version that did that. But ultimately we ended up using micromorph a light DOM diff written by Nate Moore (of Astro).

And with that, we have ported the Taste movie app demo in its 13kb of JS glory. (Thanks to a gentle nudge from Addy Osmani, and the great work of Nikhil, David, and several members of the Solid community: dev-rb, Muhammad Zaki, Paulo Riccuiti, and others).

The search page especially shows off reloading without losing client state. As you type the input doesn't lose focus even though it needs to update that whole nested panel.

Solid Movies Demo
And on Github

Just to give you an idea of how absurdly small this is. This is the total JavaScript navigating between two movie listings pages, then navigating into a movie in various frameworks with client-side routing from https://tastejs.com/movies/.

Note: Only the Solid demo is using server rendered partials so it is a bit of an unequal comparison. But the point is to emphasize the difference in size. Other frameworks are working on similar solutions, things like RSCs in Next and Containers in Qwik, but these are the demos that are available today.

Conclusion

The more apps we build this way, the more excited I am about the technology. It feels like a Single Page App in every way yet it's considerably smaller. Honestly, I surprise myself every time I open the network tab.

We're still working on moving this out of experimental and solidifying the APIs. And there is more room to optimize on the server rendering side, but we think there are all the makings of a new sort of architecture here. And that's pretty cool.
item.author: ryansolid

item.title: 音乐星期一——你在听什么？ (Funk Edition) （放克版） | Music Monday — What are you listening to? (Funk Edition)
item.description:

如果音乐讨论没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

item.author: michaeltharrington

item.title: 什么是 VPN？ 我应该选择哪一个？ | What is a VPN? Which one should I choose?
item.description:
有时，即使是最好的互联网连接也会出现问题。 拥有 VPN 不仅有助于保护隐私和安全，还可以用于诊断互联网连接问题。 从某种意义上说，它是虚拟的，该技术最终成为 Internet 上的 LAN，但实际数据是通过有线和 IP 发送的。 它的外观和功能可能与 LAN 完全相同，但端点位于 WAN 或“广域网”上。

选择 VPN 时，您不仅要选择正确的东西，而且要选择不会与您尝试做的事情完全相反的东西，即提供与互联网的替代但安全的连接。 在受感染的网络上连接到一些不太安全的粗略网络几乎没有意义。 一些已经存在一段时间了，大多数都是一样的，我会在一分钟内选择哪一个。 现在，我将解释 VPN 的作用。

对于那些被欺骗相信 VPN 会将您隐藏在外部世界之外的人，您错了。 例如，您的 ISP 屏蔽了 DNS 查询。 如果您的互联网服务提供商选择监控您，那么如果您使用他们的 DNS 服务器，这将非常容易。 更重要的是，查询通常以纯文本形式发送。 他们可以很容易地告诉你去哪些网站。 使用 VPN 可以防止您的 ISP 窥探。 这还会导致他们在尝试利用您的信息时使用不太直接的来源。 他们将不得不检查分析而不是实时域名查询。

使用 VPN 还可以保护您的隐私。 通过屏蔽您的 IP 地址，您在线访问的网站和服务器将不知道您的连接来自何处。 它使用 VPN 的 IP 地址向服务器隐藏您的源连接 IP 地址。 这意味着，如果您没有使用您的 IP 地址，当您断开 VPN 与游戏的连接时，您将不太可能在玩在线游戏时受到离线攻击。 没有什么比在当晚晚些时候被攻击并且无法流式传输 NetFlix 的游戏中表现出色的了。 过去发生了很多。 此外，如果您只是在线，您就是目标，您不一定要做得很好。 如果你有一个相当困难和限制性的 ISP 喜欢阻止你观看某些节目或阻止网站，那么 VPN 也可以保护你免受这种情况的影响。 例如，酒店有时只会让您做某些事情，连接 VPN 可以不受限制地访问并通过加密保护您的所有信息。

选择最佳 VPN 很简单。 我真正推荐的只有一个，那就是 Proton VPN。 第二个选择是cyber ghost，第三个是nord。 我不推荐后者，因为我注意到跟踪正在进行。 即使在折扣定价选项下，我也不认为您应该以这种方式被跟踪。 它从未被完全披露，这可能只意味着一件事，即对用户的不尊重。 普腾不一样。 他们尽最大努力提供优质、真实的服务器。 我有高水平的攻击，我注意到质子是唯一能够适应的。 他们的安全是一流的。 该技术只是工作，我真的不能要求更多。 我对此非常满意。

最好的选择，创建你自己的。 购买 VPS、虚拟专用服务器，并学习如何创建 VPN 服务器并自己制作。 为了安全起见，您真的不需要跳不同的 Ips。 如果可以的话，如果您打算认真对待安全性，那么创建自己的至关重要。 拥有一个可以拨入的远程访问点会有所帮助。 如果你是普通用户？ 坚持质子。 它简单、安全、有效，而且我一直在使用它。 他们还提供一流的安全电子邮件。 免费。

在选择选项时，最好选择哪些选项？

最肯定的是，TCP over UDP。 使用 UDP 可以提高速度，但它可能会受到攻击。 此外，选择 IKEv2 而不是 OpenVPN。 当您无法连接时，请使用隐身选项。 我不太确定，但这足以引起警报，所以我必须用星号标记。 然而，大多数 OpenVPN 配置都是使用 Linux 的网络管理器完成的，因此您只需右键单击网络图标即可连接。

我最喜欢的测试 VPN 连接的方法之一是阅读国外的新闻。 确保清除 cookie 并使用正确的搜索引擎。 例如，谷歌和 Youtube 在中国不存在，除非他们使用 VPN。 然而，此时，谷歌中国正在进入，谷歌似乎正在重返中国。 他们可能需要清理体面法。

谢谢阅读！



原文:

VPN stands for, “Virtual Private Network”. A VPN is crucial in modern day security. Sometimes, even the best internet connections have issues. Having a VPN is not only great for privacy and security, it is also used to diagnose internet connection issues. A VPN works by encompassing your internet connection into a VLAN or “Virtual Local Area Network”. It’s virtual in the sense that the technology ends up being a LAN over the internet, but the actual data is sent via the wire and through IP. It may appear and function exactly like a LAN but the endpoints are over a WAN or, “Wide Area Network”.

When choosing a VPN you not only want to choose something correct but you want to choose something you won’t do the exact opposite of what you are trying to do, and that is provide an alternate but secure connection to the internet. It makes little sense to be on a compromised network and connect to some sketchy network that is less than secure. A few have been out there for a while most are the same, I’ll go over which one to choose in a minute. For now, I will explain what a VPN does.

For those of you who are tricked into believing that a VPN hides you from the outside world, you are mistaken. The VPN does encrypt your otherwise non-encrypted data. For instance, DNS queries are shielded from your ISP. If your internet service provider chooses to monitor you, its very easy if you are using their DNS servers. Even more so, queries are often sent in plain text. They can tell what sites you go to very easily. Using a VPN prevents your ISP from snooping. It also causes them to use sources that are less immediate when trying to exploit your information. They will have to check analytics instead of live domain name queries.

Using a VPN also protects your privacy. By shielding your IP address, websites and servers you visit online will be unaware where your connection is coming from. It hides your source connection IP address from servers by using the VPN’s IP address. This means, playing online games you will be less likely to be attacked offline when you disconnect your VPN from the game providing you did not use your IP address. There is nothing like doing really well on a game to be attacked and unable to stream NetFlix later on that evening. It’s happened a lot in the past. Also if you are simply online you are a target, you don’t necessarily have to do well. A VPN prevents this.

If you have a rather difficult and restrictive ISP that likes to block you from watching certain shows, or blocks sites, a VPN can shield you from this as well. For instance, a hotel will only let you do certain things sometimes, connecting a VPN allows unrestricted access and protects all your information via encryption.

Choosing the best VPN is simple. There is only one I truly recommend and that is Proton VPN. The second choice is cyber ghost and third is nord. I do not recommend the latter because I’ve noticed tracking going on. Even under the discounted pricing options, I don’t believe you should be tracked this way. It was never fully disclosed and that can mean only one thing, disrespect to the users. Proton is different. They try their best and provide quality, real servers. I’ve had high level attacks, I’ve noticed proton is the only one able to adapt. Their security is top notch. The technology just works, and I really can’t ask for more. I’m super happy with it.

The best option, create your own. Buy a VPS, virtual private server, and learn how to create a VPN server and make your own. You don’t really need to hop different Ips for security. If you can, creating your own is crucial if you are planning to be serious about security. Having a remote access point you can dial into helps. If you are a regular user? Stick with proton. It’s easy, safe, works, and I’ve been using it forever. They also provide top notch secure email. For free.

When selecting the options what are the best ones to choose?

Most definitely, TCP over UDP. Using UDP is okay for speed, but it can be attacked. Also, choosing IKEv2 over OpenVPN. IKEv2 has been around forever. IKEv2 is the best bet. When you cannot connect, use the stealth option. Wireguard is GREAT for gaming, but I’ve noticed they can switch you off it, and back to regular net and back again without getting you disconnected. I’m not too sure, but it was enough to raise alarm so I will have to mark that with an asterisk. OpenVPN is a fine option as well. Most OpenVPN configurations are done using the network manager with Linux, however, so you may simply right click the network icon to connect.

One of my favorite ways to test the VPN connection is by reading the news in foreign countries. Make sure to clear the cookies and use the correct search engines. For instance, Google and Youtube do not exist in China, unless they are using a VPN. However as of this time, Google China is making an entrance and it appears Google is coming back to China. They probably needed to clean up for the decency laws.

Thanks for reading!
item.author: stephen730

item.title: 我创建了一个纯 Unicode 代码荧光笔：P | I Created a pure Unicode Code Highlighter :P
item.description:
费利佩·雷加齐奥

发表于该工具称为“Unilight”。 它是一个简单的解析器和模块，可以修改字符串部分的 unicode 变体。

因此，您可以突出显示不接受丰富编辑的任何地方，只需一个接受 unicode 变体的纯字符串即可。 几乎适用于任何语言，看看，这是一个简单而纯粹的字符串哈哈：

您可以在此处突出显示您的代码：
源代码和文档在这里：
警告：

这是一个仅出于美学原因（只是想编码）的玩具项目。 我这样做是因为我想研究一些关于解析器的东西并用它做点什么。 然而，这种技术也有重要的弱点：

  1. 不支持 unicode 的系统不会显示文本

  2. 由于 unicode 变体是隐藏字符，因此字符串总是比看起来要长

  3. 解释器不会运行代码，因为他们不知道这些字符，必须对其进行清理

实际上，出于美学原因，该工具更有用，例如，您可以突出显示推文上的简单片段。 我认为这里最有趣的是：源代码及其之间的关系（如果您是初学者/中级人员，鉴于其简单性可能是一件好事），以及解析器（如果您更高级js，祝你好运）。

查看我们的经典 DEV 衬衫 - 有多种颜色可供选择。

一旦解除暂停，felipperegazio 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，feliperegazio 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

原文:

Felippe Regazio

Posted on Nov 7

The tool is called "Unilight". It is a simple parser and module that modifies unicode variation of string parts.

So you can highlight anywhere that doesn't accept rich editing, just have a pure string that accepts unicode variations. Works for almost any lang, take a look, this is a simple and pure STRING haha:

.......
// 𝕋𝕙𝕚𝕤 𝕚𝕤 𝕒𝕟 𝕖𝕩𝕒𝕞𝕡𝕝𝕖

𝗳𝘂𝗻𝗰𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 example() { 𝗰𝗼𝗻𝘀𝘁 a = "fizz"; 𝗰𝗼𝗻𝘀𝘁 b = "buzz"; 𝗿𝗲𝘁𝘂𝗿𝗻 a+b; }

.......

You can highlight your code here:
https://felippe-regazio.github.io/unilight/

Source code and documentation here:
https://github.com/felippe-regazio/unilight

Warning:

This is a toy project made for aesthetic reasons only (just wanted to code). I made it because I wanted to study a few things about parsers and do something with it. However, there are important weaknesses of this technique:

  1. Systems that do not support unicode will not display the text

  2. Since unicode variations are hidden characters, the string will always be longer than it looks

  3. Interpreters will not run the code because they don't know these chars, it will have to be sanitized

The tool is more useful for aesthetic reasons in fact, you can highlight simple snippets on a tweet, for example. I think the most interesting thing here is: The source code and the relationships between it (if you're a beginner/intermediate it might be a good thing to see given its simplicity), and the parser (if you're more advanced in js, good luck with that).

item.title: 继续为开源项目做贡献的 5 个理由 | 5 Reasons to Keep Contributing to Open-Source Projects
item.description:
如果您参加了今年的 Hacktoberfest，那么恭喜您！ 在新的开源贡献者入职过程中，您度过了最艰难的几个月。 即使您没有收到四个拉取请求，您仍然应该为您为增加开源项目活动所做的工作感到自豪。 好消息是，即使不再是 Hacktoberfest，您仍然可以为 OSS 做出贡献； 你才刚刚开始。 事实上，有很多理由继续为开源做出贡献，这里有五个。

为什么你应该为开源项目做贡献

建立您的开发人员简历

这对于寻找初级开发人员角色的早期职业开发人员来说非常重要。 由于寻找第一个角色的开发人员过度饱和，他们通常拥有非常相似的简历、项目和经验，因此脱颖而出很重要。 开源贡献展示了一些重要的技能：

  • 您从事不属于您自己的项目的能力；

  • 您与开源维护者的沟通技巧；

  • 您遵循贡献指南的程度；

  • 您的技术技能，包括您的提交信息、拉取请求和代码注释。

改善科技产业

开源项目的一大优点是它们通常会改善整个科技行业。 如果您正在为一个被广泛使用的项目做出贡献，那么您就是在帮助确保该软件是稳定的，能够随着社区的需求而增长，或者是安全的。 对于许多人来说，这降低了开发新项目的成本。 它还通过减少他们的工作量来支持开源维护者，并允许他们与可能继续成为项目核心团队一部分的贡献者建立关系。

扩展您的开发者社区

如果您是独立工作、远程工作，或者与您的队友没有很多或积极的互动，您可能没有归属感或感觉自己是社区的一员。 参与开源为成为技术社区的一部分提供了另一种途径。 与许多蓬勃发展的开源社区一起，通过合作和其他互动机会，有一种统一的目标感。 根据项目的不同，可能会有讨论板、松弛或不和谐、实时流，甚至是与维护者和其他贡献者聊天的活动。 在健康的社区中，人们愿意共同成长并为其他社区成员提供支持。

允许多样性

随着努力使开源成为一个更具包容性和欢迎空间的努力，开源的多样性也会增加。 因为它对所有贡献者开放，所以开源软件受益于拥有各种声音、背景和解决影响整个科技行业的问题的人。 培养支持性环境可以为历来在该行业中属于少数群体的开发人员提供多样性、创新和机会，从而增加代表性。 为更好地展示种族、民族、性别、社会经济和教育背景创造空间， 与对项目的积极影响相关。

提供学习体验

由于许多存储库都存在不同程度的开放问题，因此贡献者可以加深对特定技术、特定产品架构以及团队沟通和实施的理解。 还有机会与不同的技术堆栈合作，通过对有意义的项目进行动手编码来成长、学习和茁壮成长。 如果您习惯独自编码，您也可以发展您的技术和人际沟通技巧。

利用您在 Hacktoberfest 期间发现的势头，带您进入为开源软件做出贡献的一年。 如果您对 Hacktoberfest 有很好的体验，请考虑在我们的 #discuss 帖子上分享。 如果您需要帮助找到一个地方来保持这种势头，您可以查看我们在Hacktoberfest 2022 帖子中推荐的一些项目，或者将您的项目添加到我们的讨论帖子中。

查看我们的经典 DEV 衬衫 - 有多种颜色可供选择。

一旦解除暂停，deepgram 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发布，deepgram 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 deepgram 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 deepgram 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 deepgram 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

If you participated in Hacktoberfest this year, congratulations! You survived one of the biggest months in onboarding new open-source contributors. Even if you didn’t get your four pull requests in, you should still be proud of the work that you did to increase your activity in open-source projects. And the good news is that even though it’s not Hacktoberfest anymore, you can still contribute to OSS; you’re just getting started. In fact, there are a lot of reasons to continue contributing to open-source, and here are five of them.

Why You Should Contribute To Open-Source Projects

Builds Your Developer Resume

This one can be really important for early career developers looking for a junior developer role. With an oversaturation of developers looking for their first role, who often have very similar resumes, projects, and experience, it’s important to stand out. Open-source contributions demonstrate some important skills:

  • Your ability to work on projects that are not your own;

  • Your communication skills with open source maintainers;

  • How well you follow contribution guidelines;

  • Your technical skills, including your commit messages, pull requests, and code comments.

Improves the Tech Industry

One of the great things about open-source projects is that they often improve the overall tech industry. If you’re contributing to a project that’s widely used, you’re helping to ensure that the software is stable, is able to grow with the needs of the community, or is secure. And for many, this reduces the costs of developing new projects. It also supports open-source maintainers by decreasing their workload, and allowing them to cultivate relationships with contributors who may go on to become part of the core team of the project.

Expands Your Developer Community

If you’re working independently, remotely, or don’t have many or positive interactions with your teammates, you might not feel a sense of belonging or like you’re a part of a community. Getting involved in open-source provides another avenue to be a part of a tech community. With many thriving open-source communities, there’s a united sense of purpose by working together and also other opportunities to interact. Depending on the project, there may be a discussion board, slack or discord, live streams, and even events to chat with maintainers and other contributors. In healthy communities, there’s a willingness to grow together and to provide support to other community members.

Allows for Diversity

As efforts increase to make open source a more inclusive and welcoming space, there will be an increase in diversity in open source as well. Because it’s open to all contributors, open-source software benefits from having a variety of voices, backgrounds, and people approaching problems that impact all of the tech industry. Cultivating supportive environments allows for diversity, innovation, and opportunities for developers who have historically been minorities in the industry, increasing representation. Creating space for better representation of race, ethnicity, gender, socioeconomic, and educational background, correlates with positive impacts on projects.

Provides a Learning Experience

Because many repositories have issues open for varying degrees of understanding, contributors can deepen their understanding of a specific technology, the architecture of a particular product, and team communication and implementation. There’s also the opportunity to work with different tech stacks to grow, learn, and thrive through hands-on coding on meaningful projects. If you’re used to coding alone, you can develop your technical and interpersonal communication skills as well.

Hacktoberfest is an exciting time of the year for OSS, but it’s not the end. Use the momentum you found during Hacktoberfest to carry you into a year of contributing to open-source software. If you had a good experience with Hacktoberfest, consider sharing it on our #discuss post. And if you need help finding a place to keep that momentum going, you can check out some of the projects we recommended in our Hacktoberfest 2022 post or add your project to our discussion post.

Check out our classic DEV shirt — available in multiple colors.

Sebastiaan Janssen - Nov 7

item.title: Netlify、Vite、部署和“找不到页面”错误 | Netlify, Vite, deploy and “Page Not Found” error
item.description:
奥尔加·乌伦采娃

发表于我知道当他们的 Web 应用程序在 localhost 上完美运行时，很多人都在为这个问题而苦苦挣扎，但是在刷新页面后在 Netlify 服务器上他们收到错误“页面未找到”。 为什么以及如何修复它？

这个问题的关键不在于 Netlify 是如何工作的。 实际上 Netlify 或其他东西并不重要，因为它可以是另一台服务器。 问题在于，您的服务器不知道如何正确响应您的请求。 尽管我将提到 Netlify，但您应该记住，它可能是别的东西。

我们有一个 Netlify 服务器，但我们无权访问它，我们不能直接编码，如果请求到达特定地址，服务器必须提供这个特定文件。 我们根本没有这样的机会。 但是我们的 Netlify 服务器所拥有的只是包含一堆文件的dist目录。 因此，如果有对https://netlify.app/icon.svg的请求，我们的 Netlify 服务器也应该响应来自 dist 的文件 icon.svg。 但是如果我们想获取https://netlify.app/example/1怎么办？ 我们的 SPA 中确实有这条路线，并且在localhost上一切正常，但我们会收到“未找到页面”错误。 为什么？ 只是因为我们的dist目录中没有那个文件。 我们唯一拥有的是index.html ，所以我们必须设置redirects ，因为我们希望负责处理到 SPA 的路由。 因此，如果dist中没有匹配名称的文件，我们的服务器必须响应index.html 。

有很多方法可以做到这一点，当然，正如我希望你记住的那样，在下一个方法之前，Netlify 没有什么特别的。 但是，如果您使用 Vite，我也建议您阅读此公用文件夹文档。

好吧，如果我们正在考虑 Netlify，我只需要在项目目录中创建netlify.toml ：

 // netlify.toml [[redirects]] from = "/*" to = "/index.html" status = 200

就是这样！ 错误消失了。

老实说，当我无法控制服务器并且创建诸如netlify.toml之类的文件是挂钩“我们的”（不是我们的）服务器的唯一方法时，我真的不喜欢这个想法，这让我很尴尬。 但我想这是另一个指标，表明我们需要转向比 Netlify 更底层的东西，尽管它对宠物项目非常有用。

感谢阅读：）

萨曼莎玛伯格—— 一旦暂停，olgaurentseva 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，olgaurentseva 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发表，olgaurentseva 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 olgaurentseva 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 olgaurentseva 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 olgaurentseva 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Olga Urentseva

Posted on Nov 7

I know that a lot of people struggling with the problem when their web app works perfectly on localhost, but on Netlify server after refreshing page they got error “Page Not Found”. Why and how it should be fixed?

The key of this problem is not how Netlify works. Actually Netlify or something else is not matters, because it can be another server. The problem is the fact, that your server doesn’t know how to respond on your requests properly. Despite the fact that I’ll will refer to Netlify, you should keep in mind that it could be something else.

How Netlify [server] saves your files? — There is a dist directory with similar files:

dist
  index.html
  hash.js
  icon.svg
  favicon.ico

We have a Netlify server and we don’t have access to it, we cannot directly code that if a request comes to a specific address, server must give this specific file. We simply don’t have such an opportunity. But all that our Netlify server has is dist directory with bunch of files. So if there is a request to https://netlify.app/icon.svg, our Netlify server should respond with a file icon.svg from dist as well. But what if we want to get https://netlify.app/example/1? We do have this route in our SPA and everything works well on localhost, but we we’ll get “Not Found Page” error. Why? Just because there is no that file in our dist directory. Only thing that we have is index.html so we have to set up redirects, because we want to give responsibility of taking care of routes to SPA. So our server has to respond with index.html if there is no file in dist with matched name.

There is a bunch of ways to do it and of course as I want you to remember, there is nothing specific to Netlify till next approach. But if you’re using Vite I also recommend you read this Public Folder Documentation.

Well, if we’re considering Netlify, all I need is create netlify.toml in project directory:

// netlify.toml
[[redirects]]
  from = "/*"
  to = "/index.html"
  status = 200

And that’s it! Error is gone.

Honestly I don’t really like this idea when I can not control the server and creating files such as netlify.toml is the only way to hook “our” (not really our) server makes me awkward. But I guess this is another indicator that we need to move to something more low-level than Netlify, although it is really good for pet-projects.

And thanks for reading :)

samanthamarberger - Nov 7

item.title: É bom conhecer o mundo | É bom conhecer o mundo
item.description:
Eu geralmente gosto do desconforto de desconhecer algo. Eu geralmente gosto do desconforto de desconhecer 算法。 E aí eu passo a saber sobre essa coisa e eu fico totalmente enlouquecida para saber mais. E aí eu passo a sabre sobre essa coisa e eu fico totalmente enlouquecida para sabre mais。 E desde que eu comecei a trabalhar em empresas que têm todos os tipos de pessoas, eu tento absorver o máximo da cultura e das diferenças ao meu redor. E desde que eu comecei a trabalhar em empresas que têm todos os tipos de pessoas, eu tento absorver o máximo da cultura e das diferenças o meu redor。

Esses dias eu estava completamente obcecada com o Diwali . Esses dias eu estava completamente obcecada com o Diwali 。 O Diwali para quem não sabe é um festival na Índia que é conhecido por ser o festival das luzes e da felicidade. O Diwali para quem não sabe é um Festival na Índia que é conhecido por ser o Festival das luzes e da felicidade。 Todas as cidades se iluminam para comemorar a vitória da luz sobre à escuridão. Todas as cidades se iluminam para comemorar a vitória da luz sobre à escuridão。 E eu só fui capaz de saber tudo isso através dos meus coworkers, que estavam saindo viajar e se reunir com suas famílias para aproveitar o feriado e celebrar. E eu só fui capaz de saber tudo isso através dos meus coworkers, que estavam saindo viajar e se reunir com suas famílias para aproveitar o feriado e celebrar。 Muitas pessoas dividiram também sobre as suas infâncias assistindo o anime Ramayana: The Legend of Prince Rama durante o feriado. Muitas pessoas dividiram também sobre as suas infâncias assistantindo o 日本动漫Ramayana: The Legend of Rama durante o feriado.

Ah e durante o Diwali desse ano também aconteceu o grande jogo da Copa de Mundo de Cricket Paquistão x Índia, grandes rivais, pra alegrar ainda mais o feriado. Ah e durante o Diwali desse ano também aconteceu o grande jogo da Copa de Mundo de Cricket Paquistão x Índia, grandes rivais, pra alegrar ainda mais o feriado。 O Cricket na Índia é como o Futebol aqui no Brasil, e Paquistão x Índia é como um grande jogo entre Brasil e Argentina! O Cricket na Índia é como o Futebol aqui no Brasil, e Paquistão x Índia é como um grande jogo entre Brasil e Argentina！

E é bom conhecer o nosso mundo como ele é: diverso . E é bom conhecer o nosso mundo como ele é: diverso 。 Mas às vezes também incomoda muito porque no mundo existem diferenças que não são tão lindas , mas te faz conhecer pessoas incríveis. Mas às vezes também incomoda muito porque no mundo existem diferenças que não são tão lindas , mas te faz conhecer pessoas incríveis。 Mulheres incríveis como a que eu conheci hoje: Meital Avital . Mulheres incríveis como a que eu conheci hoje： Meital Avital 。

Meital é uma manager na Red Hat e ela é de Israel. Meital é uma 经理 na Red Hat e ela é de Israel。 Mas além de uma manager incrível, que se preocupa até em escolher as palavras corretas para conversar com cada uma das pessoas diferentes que ela gerencia, ela também é uma mulher. Mas além de uma manager incrível, que se preocupa até em escolher as palavras corretas para conversar com cada uma das pessoas diferentes que ela gerencia, ela também é uma mulher。 Uma mulher israelita que lida com coisas que acontecem no próprio país dela com todas as mulheres. Uma mulher israelita que lida com coisas que acontecem no próprio país dela com todas as mulheres。

E então ela me contou: há 6 anos, o marido dela fugiu do país, abandonando ela e as crianças. E então ela me contou：há 6 anos，o marido dela fugiu do país，Bandandando ela e as crianças。 "Ela vai se divorciar né", a gente pensa. “Ela vai se divorciar né”，一种绅士风格。 Mas então ela me conta que por mais que ela queira muito se divorciar, ela como mulher não tem o direito de pedir o divórcio. Mas então ela me conta que por mais que ela queira muito se divorciar, ela como mulher não tem o direito de pedir o divórcio。 É isso mesmo, ela está presa num relacionamento em que nenhum dos dois quer estar. É isso mesmo, ela está presa num relacionamento em que nenhum dos dois quer estar。

Mas ela não se contentou por aí: ela começou a militar pelo direito das mulheres em Israel. Mas ela não se contentou por aí: ela começou amilitary pelo direito das mulheres em Israel。 Ela se tornou uma voz para as mulheres, num país em que o serviço militar é obrigatório para as mulheres, mas que não dá à elas o direito de se divorciar. Ela se tornou uma voz para as mulheres, num país em que o serviçomilaré obrigatório para as mulheres, mas que não dá à elas o direito de se divorciar。 E a voz dela tá fazendo efeito: ela já foi capaz de mudar até uma lei em Israel, que permite que mulheres divorciadas possam ter mais filhos após o casamento. E a voz dela tá fazendo efeito: ela já foi capaz de mudar até uma lei em Israel, que permite que mulheres divorciadas possam ter mais filhos após o casamento。

E o que isso tem a ver com tecnologia? E o que isso tem a ver com tecnologia？ Nossos times são formados por pessoas e se o teu time for global como o meu, a cultura de cada uma dessas pessoas influencia em como elas são, o que elas fazem eo que elas acreditam. Nossos times são formados por pessoas e se o teu time for global como o meu, a cultura de cada uma dessas pessoas influencia em como elas são, o que elas fazem eo que elas acreditam。 E um time só existe se relações existirem. E um time so existe se relaçõesexistirem。 Mas relações reais, por isso que eu absorvo tudo. Mas relações reais, por isso que eu absorvo tudo。 Absorvo do Cricket até a injustiça de uma mulher incrível não poder se divorciar. Absorvo do Cricket até a injustiça de uma mulher incrível não poder se divorciar。

Aprendo muito sobre o mundo, mas principalmente sobre as pessoas, porque tecnologia sempre será sobre as pessoas. Aprendo muito sobre o mundo, mas principalmente sobre as pessoas, porque tecnologia semper será sobre as pessoas。



原文:

Eu geralmente gosto do desconforto de desconhecer algo. E aí eu passo a saber sobre essa coisa e eu fico totalmente enlouquecida para saber mais. E desde que eu comecei a trabalhar em empresas que têm todos os tipos de pessoas, eu tento absorver o máximo da cultura e das diferenças ao meu redor.

Esses dias eu estava completamente obcecada com o Diwali. O Diwali para quem não sabe é um festival na Índia que é conhecido por ser o festival das luzes e da felicidade. Todas as cidades se iluminam para comemorar a vitória da luz sobre à escuridão. E eu só fui capaz de saber tudo isso através dos meus coworkers, que estavam saindo viajar e se reunir com suas famílias para aproveitar o feriado e celebrar. Muitas pessoas dividiram também sobre as suas infâncias assistindo o anime Ramayana: The Legend of Prince Rama durante o feriado.

Ah e durante o Diwali desse ano também aconteceu o grande jogo da Copa de Mundo de Cricket Paquistão x Índia, grandes rivais, pra alegrar ainda mais o feriado. O Cricket na Índia é como o Futebol aqui no Brasil, e Paquistão x Índia é como um grande jogo entre Brasil e Argentina!

E é bom conhecer o nosso mundo como ele é: diverso. Mas às vezes também incomoda muito porque no mundo existem diferenças que não são tão lindas , mas te faz conhecer pessoas incríveis. Mulheres incríveis como a que eu conheci hoje: Meital Avital.

Meital é uma manager na Red Hat e ela é de Israel. Mas além de uma manager incrível, que se preocupa até em escolher as palavras corretas para conversar com cada uma das pessoas diferentes que ela gerencia, ela também é uma mulher. Uma mulher israelita que lida com coisas que acontecem no próprio país dela com todas as mulheres.

E então ela me contou: há 6 anos, o marido dela fugiu do país, abandonando ela e as crianças. "Ela vai se divorciar né", a gente pensa. Mas então ela me conta que por mais que ela queira muito se divorciar, ela como mulher não tem o direito de pedir o divórcio. É isso mesmo, ela está presa num relacionamento em que nenhum dos dois quer estar.

Mas ela não se contentou por aí: ela começou a militar pelo direito das mulheres em Israel. Ela se tornou uma voz para as mulheres, num país em que o serviço militar é obrigatório para as mulheres, mas que não dá à elas o direito de se divorciar. E a voz dela tá fazendo efeito: ela já foi capaz de mudar até uma lei em Israel, que permite que mulheres divorciadas possam ter mais filhos após o casamento.

E o que isso tem a ver com tecnologia? Nossos times são formados por pessoas e se o teu time for global como o meu, a cultura de cada uma dessas pessoas influencia em como elas são, o que elas fazem e o que elas acreditam. E um time só existe se relações existirem. Mas relações reais, por isso que eu absorvo tudo. Absorvo do Cricket até a injustiça de uma mulher incrível não poder se divorciar.

Aprendo muito sobre o mundo, mas principalmente sobre as pessoas, porque tecnologia sempre será sobre as pessoas.
item.author: andressadotpy

item.title: 经验教训：#TechIsHiring 的故事第 3 部分 | The lessons learned: The Story of #TechIsHiring part 3
item.description:
乍得河斯图尔特

发表于在上一篇文章中，我们讨论了构建 TechIsHiring 当前价值的工作以及运行标签带来的一些障碍。

得到教训

尽管运行 TechIsHiring 存在任何问题，但运行和维护它一直是我为我的职业生涯做出的最佳决定之一。 我通过维护这个结识了很多人，并且由于我与主题标签的关联，我被要求就几个主题发表演讲。 这一切都源于愿意花一些时间出去帮助人们寻找机会，即使这只是意味着在 Twitter 上闲逛，寻找推文并转发它们。

我认为我从 TechIsHiring 中学到了一些重要的经验教训，它的维护非常适用于其他试图打入科技行业的人：

  • 回馈： TechIsHiring 真正教会我的一件事是，建立你的职业生涯的最佳方式之一就是回馈科技社区。 回馈社区有助于让你的名字出现在那里，并且以一种积极的方式这样做。 人们会看到这一点，并且会更有兴趣接触并提供您的机会。 老实说，我确实得到了比我认为我真正应得的更多的机会。 不过需要注意的是，您需要在公共场合回馈。 你不能在角落里研究“下一件大事”，然后期望所有这些伟大的事情在你发布后就会发生。 人们对旅程的反应比对目的地的反应更多，所以让其他人跟随你的旅程。 你会很高兴你做到了！

  • 您不必从大做起： TechIsHiring 最初只是一个 Twitter 主题标签，仅此而已。 没有帐户或时事通讯或任何东西。 我刚拿到手机，我转发了我找到的工作。 我今天仍然这样做。 您如何回馈不需要详细说明，也不需要成为一件大事。 做一些人们认为有益的事情，你会惊讶于人们会对你做出积极的反应！

  • 寻求反馈：对上一点的快速跟进是积极寻求反馈。 在您工作时，请与人们联系并听取他们对您正在做什么以及如何做得更好的意见。 如果没有几个只对帮助我做得更好的人的反馈，TechIsHiring 就不会成为今天的样子。 如果您已经做了一段时间，人们会更愿意给您一些反馈并帮助您，只要能够说他们是您旅程的一部分！

  • 不要低估您所追求的技术需求：此建议专门针对那些正在或正在寻求工程职位的人。 我之前已经说过几次了，但 TechIsHiring 只是一个标签。 从那时起，我构建了一个 Twitter 机器人，我必须对其进行更新，并为 TechIsHiring 构建了一个网站。 所有这些解决方案都需要部署在某种形式的基础设施上以保持它们的运行。 当我在 TechIsHiring 工作时，我担心的一个问题是没有太多的技术需求，因为我只是用标签转发推文。 从那以后，我不得不带来相当多的软件工程技能和经验来解决 TechIsHiring 面临的一些技术问题。 不要低估你决定追求的任何技术需求。 有很多关于某人通过周末项目解决特定问题的故事，这些项目变成了他们的日常工作，甚至是他们一生的工作。 您会惊讶地发现，“我需要一个网站”这样简单的事情如何能够迅速发展为有趣且引人入胜的技术解决方案！ 只要记住在公共场合做这项工作！

  • 坚持不懈： TechIsHiring 教会我的最重要的事情是坚持不懈的价值。 每天露面，无论完成的工作质量如何，都比只露面并做一次绝对最好的工作要好得多！ 这是一个缓慢的磨练，并且继续是一个缓慢的磨练，但坚持不懈肯定会有所回报。 让自己能够始终如一地投入某种形式的工作，随着时间的推移，这些工作会自行复合。 如果您对此一无所获，请拿走那个！

结论

就是这样。 这就是我如何让每个人都知道 TechIsHiring 的故事！ 这是一段有趣的旅程，老实说，我没想到我最终会成为这样的一部分。 但我真的很享受这次旅程，并期待看到这段旅程带来了什么！

  • 如果您觉得这篇文章很有趣，请随时关注这篇文章！
  • 如果您有兴趣了解有关前端工程的更多信息，请在Dev.to和Twitter上关注我。
  • 如果您正在寻找工作，我强烈建议您在Twitter 、 LinkedIn或 TechIsHiring 的新网站https://www.TechIsHiring.com/上查看 @TechIsHiring 以获取已发布的工作和其他资源！

亚当纳撒尼尔戴维斯 - 伊丽莎白布朗 - 一旦暂停，chad_r_stewart 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，chad_r_stewart 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，chad_r_stewart 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 chad_r_stewart 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 chad_r_stewart 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 chad_r_stewart 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Chad R. Stewart

Posted on Nov 7

In the last article, we talked about the work that went into building the current value of TechIsHiring and a few hurdles that comes from running a hashtag.

Lessons Learned

Despite any problems with running TechIsHiring, running and maintaining this has been one of the best decisions I’ve made for my career. I’ve met so many people through maintaining this and have been asked to speak on several topics because of my association with the hashtag. And it all really stemmed from being willing to spend some time to go out and help people find opportunity, even if that just meant poking around Twitter and finding tweets and just retweeting them.

I think there are a few key lessons I took from TechIsHiring and it’s maintenance that is very applicable to others trying to break into the Tech Industry:

  • Give Back: One of the things TechIsHiring has really taught me that one of the best ways to build your career is to give back to the Tech Community. Giving back to the community helps put your name out there and does so in a way that’s positive. People will see that and will be more interested in reaching out and offering your opportunities. I’ve definitely been offered much more opportunities than I think I really deserved honestly. One caveat though, you need to be giving back in public. You can’t be in a corner working on ‘the next big thing’ and then expect all of this great stuff to happen once you release it. People respond to the journey more than the destination so let others follow you along on that journey. You’ll be so glad you did!

  • You don’t have to start big: TechIsHiring started as a Twitter hashtag, nothing more. There was no account or newsletter or anything. I just had my phone and I retweeted jobs I found. I still do this today. How you give back doesn’t need to be elaborate or needs to be this great big thing. Do something that people find beneficial and you’ll be surprised how much people will react positively to you!

  • Seek feedback: A quick follow up to the previous point is actively for feedback. As you’re working, reach out to people and get their opinion on what you’re doing and how you could be doing better. TechIsHiring wouldn’t be the way it is today without the feedback of several people who were only interested in helping me do better. If you’ve been doing something for a while, people will be much more willing to give you some feedback and help you along, if only to be able to say they were a part of your journey!

  • Don’t underestimate the technical need for what you pursue: This advice is specifically for those in or are seeking Engineering roles. I’ve said this a few times before but TechIsHiring was just a hashtag starting out. Since then, I’ve built a Twitter bot which I had to update and built a website for TechIsHiring. All of these solutions needed to be deployed on some form of infrastructure to keep them running. A concern I had as I worked on TechIsHiring is that there wasn’t much of a technical need since I was just retweeting with a hashtag. I’ve since had to bring a fair amount of my Software Engineering skills and experience to solve some of the technical problems TechIsHiring has faced. Don’t underestimate the technical need for whatever you decide to pursue. There are many stories of someone solving a particular problem with a weekend project that turned into their day job or even their life’s work. You’d be surprised how something so simple as, “I need a website,” can balloon to interesting and engaging technical solutions! Just remember to do this work in public!

  • Be persistent: The most important thing that TechIsHiring has taught me was the value of being persistent. Showing up every day, regardless of the quality of the work done, is significantly better than showing up and doing your absolute best work ONLY ONCE! This has been a slow grind and continues to be a slow grind but being persistent definitely pays off. Set yourself up to be able to put in some form of work consistently and over time that work will compound on itself. If you take nothing away from this, please take that!

Conclusion

And that’s really it. That’s the story of how I came to let everyone know that TechIsHiring! It’s been an interesting journey and I honestly did not think I would have ended up being a part of something like this. But I really am enjoying the ride and looking forward to seeing what this journey brings!

  • If you found this article interesting, please feel free to heart this article!
  • If you’re interested in learning more about Front-End Engineering, follow me here on Dev.to and Twitter.
  • If you’re looking for jobs, I’d highly recommend checking out @TechIsHiring on Twitter, LinkedIn or TechIsHiring's new website https://www.TechIsHiring.com/ for posted jobs and other resources!

Adam Nathaniel Davis - Apr 9 '21

Elizabeth Brown - Apr 16 '21

LuisMejiaDev - Mar 22 '21

item.title: 成为自学成才的 10 种方法 | Top 10 ways to become a self-taught developer
item.description:
各位新手和自学成才的开发者大家好，作为一个菜鸟，你在学习中遇到了哪些挑战？

作为一名自学成才的开发者，我面临的最大挑战是：

  • 没有明确的路径和愿景
  • 缺乏结构
  • 没有人追究我的责任
  • 没有适当的指导

我一个人学习。 没有人在那里指导我或分享我的问题和挫折。 每当我感到困惑和筋疲力尽时，我都会拖延几天甚至几周来冷静下来。
你听起来很熟悉吗？

我是通过艰难的方式学会的，但你不必经历我所经历的。 或者至少你可以避免把时间花在我希望我早点知道并且可以做得更好的事情上。
我希望这个建议可以帮助和指导你作为新的自学开发者。

作为一名自学成才的开发人员和新手，我对几个不同的开发领域产生了兴趣。
我正处于选择追求数据科学或网络开发的十字路口。

我首先学习了 Python，因为我听说它是​​一种对初学者友好的语言。 那么会出什么问题呢？
我花了将近两个月的时间，而且我没有超过 20% 的编程，因为它没有点击。 我很沮丧。

我没有坚持我的学习，而是转向了另一门课程。 我是一个视觉的人。 因此，我第一次在页面上看到用 HTML 呈现的“Hello World”这一事实让我兴奋不已。

在尝试了几种语言之后，我发现了我更大的兴趣所在。

如果您还没有找到自己的利基市场，您可以阅读此开发者路线图，让您更清楚地了解自己。

在尝试了几种语言并找到了你的利基之后，坚持并深入研究它。 擅长它。 由于成为一名成功的程序员需要花费大量的时间和精力，因此，请尽可能深入，并尝试成为某个部分或一种语言的大师。

一旦你找到最适合你的，然后从它开始。

我开始独自学习编码。 那时，早上或白天学习几乎是不可能的。 所以在她午睡时间之外，我找到了另一个适合我的时间。 房子里的每个人都睡了之后，大约是晚上 10:00。 大多数时候，我已经筋疲力尽了。 但我会鼓励自己坐下来学习至少半小时，通常会增加到一两个小时。

对我有用的学习结构是练习我前一天学到的东西。 然后我每天添加一两个新主题，而不是死记硬背一些新知识。

是的，不要死记硬背代码，只需尝试了解代码的工作原理和内部行为，这样您就会获得深入的知识。 很高兴知道：高级开发人员仍然会在谷歌上搜索东西。 因为没有人知道一切。

尝试理解概念和流程。

谷歌剩下的，还有语法，当你迷路时，就像在编程世界中每个人每天都在谷歌上搜索以找到他/她的答案，所以这不是问题。

我不喜欢问问题，因为我觉得我的问题是“非常初学者”或者是“愚蠢的问题”。 或者我可以得到，“你用谷歌搜索了吗？”。
我几乎不知道那些想法只是在我的脑海里玩。

我花了很长时间才提出我的第一个问题。 当我终于做到了，它并没有我想象的那么糟糕。

我第一次问问题时，我把它扔到了推特上。 令我惊讶的是，我收到了答案，有些人甚至提出要和我一起浏览代码。

我参加的课程为他们的学生提供了 Discord 频道。 当我需要时，我开始更有信心提出问题或寻求帮助。 所以不要犹豫！

当您卡住一段时间时，请寻求帮助。

它可能在 Twitter 或您所在的 Discord 频道上。
这个旅程应该是愉快的，让你成长，而不是让你沮丧太久甚至放弃。

学习后总是给出的一条建议是，“去构建一个项目！”。 自学成才常常陷入教程地狱。

这是一个很好的实践，我们在教程中编写代码，而不是只看不做。

但是当谈到从头开始构建项目时，有时我们甚至不知道从哪里开始。 然后我们发现自己回到了教程。

当您对一个项目有想法时，请构建它并随时随地学习。
但是，当您不知道要构建什么时，请尝试复制教程的项目而不进行回放。 更好的是，尝试为项目添加更多功能。 当你遇到困难时，用谷歌搜索你的问题以找到解决方案。

给自己半到一小时的时间来搜索它。 只有在您在那个时间范围内搜索并且仍然找不到任何解决方案之后，才寻求帮助或回顾教程。
不要让自己迷失太久而失去动力。

另一个提示，当您构建项目时，将任务分成更小的块将帮助您快速完成项目，并且您不会感到无聊，因为这种技术在 DSA 中称为分而治之。

您的目标是在 Web 开发领域找到一份工作吗？
然后，在您进一步学习之前，您可以更好地研究您所在地区的工作需求。

我的目标是找到一份全栈开发人员的工作。
我通过学习课程的课程——没有前端框架的 HTML、CSS、JavaScript、MongoDB、Express 和 NodeJS。

六个月后我完成了这门课程，有人建议我对我所在地区的网络开发人员工作进行研究。
他们说这将帮助我选择我需要学习的前端框架，因为我还没有发现。

结果令人心碎！
我所在领域的按需前端工作是 React 和 Angular，而后端是 Java、PHP 和 .NET (C#)。

根据我的研究，我学到的后端堆栈不是按需提供的。 因此，必须稍微改变计划。

当你在一个积极和支持的社区中时，你会比独自一人获得更多。

您有真正关心您并支持您的旅程的人。 您可以提出问题，在需要时获得帮助，并让整个社区对您负责。

您还可以以任何方式帮助需要您支持的其他人。
通过社区，您将获得信心并更快地成长。

学习编码对每个人来说都很难。 有时你很难理解一个概念。 很多时候你很难找到解决方案。 你可能会遇到冒名顶替综合症。

当他们来的时候，不要对自己太苛刻。

休息一下。 拍拍自己的背。 请记住，您今天的位置比昨天的位置领先一步。 不要把自己和别人比较。 唯一的比较是你现在和以前是谁和在哪里。

善待自己，永远。

要有耐心和一致，因为成为冠军需要大量的时间和精力，因为没有人会在一天之内成为大师。 建立一个伟大的人格需要时间。
只要相信这个过程。

希望以上提示对您有所帮助，祝您学习愉快😊。

随意在下面的评论中添加您作为自学开发人员的技巧和经验。
谢谢阅读。

我们店里最受欢迎的商品是贴纸。 今天拿一包（免费送货）！

原文:

Hello fellow newbies and self-taught developers, As a rookie, what are your challenges in learning?

My biggest challenges as a self-taught in development were:

  • No clear path and vision
  • Lack of structures
  • No one to hold me accountable
  • No proper guidance

I was learning alone. No one was there to guide me or share my problems and frustrations. Whenever I get confused and burned out, I procrastinate for days, even weeks, to cool down.
Does it sound familiar to you?

I learned it the hard way, but you don't have to go through what I've been through. Or at least you can avoid spending your time on things that I wish I knew sooner and could do better.
I hope this advice can help and guide you as new self-taught developers.

1. Find your field of interest.

As a self-taught developer and a newbie, I got interested in a few different development fields.
I was at crossroads between choosing to pursue data science or web development.

I learned Python first because I heard that it is a beginner-friendly language. So what can go wrong?
It took me almost two months, and I didn't go far beyond 20% of the programming because it didn't click. And I got frustrated.

Rather than stick to my learning, I switched to the other course. I am a visual person. So the fact that I could see "Hello World" rendered on the page for the first time with HTML made my heart jump with excitement.

After trying a couple of languages, I found where my bigger interest lies.

You can read this developer roadmap to give you clearer insight if you haven't found your niche.

2. Stick to your choice and go deep

After trying several languages and finding your niche, stick and go deep into it. Get good at it. As it takes a lot of time and effort to be a successful programmer so, go deep as you can and try to become a master at one portion or a language.

Once you find the best fit for you then start with it.

3. Find your best time to learn and create a structure

I started to learn to code alone. At that time, learning in the morning or during the day was almost impossible. So outside her nap time, I found another time that works for me. And it is around 10:00 PM after everybody in the house sleeps. Most of the time, I'm already exhausted. But I will intrigue myself to sit and learn for at least half-hour, which usually will increase to one or two hours.

And the learning structure that works for me is to practice what I've learned the day before. Then I add one or two new topics daily instead of cramming some new knowledge.

4. Don't memorize the code just understand how things work

Yes, don't memorize the code just try to understand how code works and internal behaviour so you will get a deep knowledge. Good to know: Senior developers would still google things. Because no one knows everything.

Try to understand the concept and the flow.

And google the rest, also the syntaxes, when you get lost as in the programming world every individual does googling every day to find his/her answers so, that’s not the issue.

5. Ask questions or help

I was uncomfortable asking questions because I felt like my questions were "very beginner" or it was "stupid questions." Or I could get, "Have you Google it?".
Little did I know that those thoughts were only playing in my head.

It took me a long time to finally threw my first question. And when I finally did, it wasn't as bad as I thought.

The first time I asked a question, I threw it on Twitter. To my surprise, I received answers, and some people even offered to walk through the codes with me.

The courses that I took provide Discord channels for their students. I started to feel more confident in asking questions or asking for help when I needed it there. So don't hesitate!

When you get stuck for some time, ask for help.

It could be on Twitter or on the Discord channels where you at.
This journey is supposed to be enjoyable, make you grow, and not make you frustrated for too long or even quit.

6. Create projects

A piece of advice that's always been given after learning something is, "Go build a project!". Self-taught is often get trapped in tutorial hell.

It's a good practice that we code along with a tutorial rather than only watching and doing nothing.

But when it comes to building a project from scratch, there would be times when we don't even know where to start. Then we find ourselves getting back to a tutorial.

When you have an idea for a project, build it and learn things on the go.
But when you don't know what to build, try replicating the tutorial's project without playing it back. Even better, try to add some more features to the project. When you get stuck, google your problems to find solutions.

Give yourself half to one hour to search for it. And only after you searched in that time frame and still don't find any solution, ask for help or look back at the tutorial.
Don't let yourself get lost for too long and get demotivated.

Another tip, when you build a project, breaking tasks into smaller chunks would help you to finish your project quickly and you won't feel bored as this technique is called Divide and Conquer in DSA.

7. Research for Jobs related to your skills

Is your goal to land a job in the web development field?
Then, before you go much further in your learning, you better research job demands in your area.

My goal is to land a job as a full-stack developer.
I learned by following the course's lessons — HTML, CSS, JavaScript, MongoDB, Express, and NodeJS without a front-end framework.

After I finished the course six months later, someone gave me the advice to do my research on web developer jobs in my area.
They said it would help me pick which front-end framework I need to learn since I haven't discovered one.

The result was heartbreaking!
The on-demand front-end jobs in my area are React and Angular, while the backend is Java, PHP, and .NET (C#).

Based on my research, the backend stack that I've learned wasn't on-demand. So a slight change of plan has to be made.

React is what I'm learning now to be a front-end developer.

8. Find a popular and active community

When you are in a positive and supportive community, you will gain much more than if you are alone.

You have people who genuinely care for you and support your journey. You can ask questions, get help when needed, and have the whole community hold you accountable.

You can also help others who need your support in any way.
With community, you will gain confidence and grow faster.

9. Be patient and kind to yourself

Learning to code is hard for everyone. There would be times when you have a hard time understanding a concept. There would be lots of times when you have trouble finding solutions. You might cross paths with imposter syndrome.

When they come, don't be too hard on yourself.

Take a break. Pat yourself on the back. Remember, where you are today is one step ahead of where you were yesterday. Don't compare yourself with anybody else. The only comparison is who and where you are now and before.

Be kind to yourself, always.

10. Last but not the least

Be patient and consistent as it takes a lot of time and effort to be a champ as no one becomes a master in one day. It takes time to build a great personality.
Just trust the process.

I hope you find the above tips helpful, and I wish you a pleasant journey in your learning 😊.

Feel free to add your tips and experience as a self-taught developer in the comment below.
Thanks for reading.

item.title: 四个星期的冲刺有什么不好？ | What's so bad about four-week sprints?
item.description:
苏格兰人

发表于在过去的十年中，我曾在各种使用 Scrum 的软件团队中工作过。 其中一些团队使用两周冲刺，一些使用四周冲刺。 与我交谈过的大多数软件团队都隐含地认为，较短的 sprint 是健康和 Scrum“成熟”的标志。 为期四周的 sprint 是针对在练习 Scrum 时仍是“孩子”的软件团队。 主要的信念是，这些团队应该有一天会变得更好，然后进行为期两周的冲刺。

不过，我个人的经验是，具有两周冲刺的团队会承担更多的工作，承受来自管理层的更高的截止日期压力，最糟糕的是，他们将更多的时间花在冲刺仪式上，而不是实施和部署代码。 两周的 sprint 包括每隔一周召开三次长会议（sprint 演示、sprint 回顾和 sprint 计划），这是在会议上花费的时间是那些每 4 周召开一次会议的人的两倍。

最后，我观察到更直接地交付 sprint 承诺的能力取决于团队的工作有多少依赖于其他团队，而不是独立交付。 工作“独立交付”能力的更大驱动力是工程部门的整体组织。 能够独立完成工作的团队天生就能够更频繁地交付小批量的工作，这与他们的 Scrum“成熟度”无关。 所以我的结论是，如果团队在两周的冲刺中遇到“麻烦”，那么工程部门可能应该重新组织他们的团队，而不是鞭打“你不够敏捷”的马。

那么，您如何看待冲刺长度？

四个星期的冲刺有什么不好？

原文:

scottshipp

Posted on Nov 7

I've worked in various software teams that use scrum for the past ten years. Some of these teams use two-week sprints and some use four-week sprints. Most software teams I've talked to implicitly believe that shorter sprints are a sign of health and scrum "maturity." Four-week-long sprints are for software teams that are still "children" at practicing scrum. The dominant belief is that those teams should get better one day and move to two-week sprints.

My personal experience, though, is that teams with two-week sprints carry over more work, experience higher deadline pressure from management, and, worst of all, spend a higher ratio of their time in sprint ceremonies rather than implementing and deploying code. Two-week sprints involve three long meetings (sprint demo, sprint retro, and sprint planning) every other week, which is twice as much time spent in meetings as those who only have those meetings every four weeks.

Finally, I have observed that the ability to deliver sprint commitments more directly depends on how much of a team's work is dependent on other teams as opposed to being independently deliverable. The larger driver of the ability for work to be "independently deliverable" is the overall organization of the engineering department. Teams that can complete their work independently are just inherently able to deliver smaller batches of work more often, and it has nothing to do with their scrum "maturity." So my takeaway is that engineering departments who have teams experiencing "trouble" with two-week sprints should probably re-org their teams, rather than flog the "you aren't agile enough" horse.

So, what do you think about sprint length?

What's so bad about four-week sprints?

item.title: 回顾：在家工作，第 3 年 | Review: Working from Home, Year 3
item.description:
这也出现在Artisan Insights博客上。

在撰写本文时，我正在为一家远程公司在家工作一周年。 由于 WFH 近来经常出现在新闻中，因此我受邀坦率地分享我对远程工作生活方式的个人看法，以及这种安排对我作为知识工作者的生活的主要方式。 以下是我自己的经历和看法。

我已经 30 多岁了，我的大部分 IT 职业生涯都是在办公楼的隔间里度过的。 我现在是一名技术顾问，在家中从事各种客户项目。 一开始我不确定会发生什么，但在我的家庭办公室工作了两年半之后，我开始充分体会到远程工作生活方式的好处。

让我们先从显而易见的开始：

通勤“Sch-Moot”

实际上，我仍然有一个“日常通勤”——有点。 我所做的就是用不同的日常生活代替通勤上班，我稍后会谈到。 现在的不同之处在于，我已经消除了典型通勤的常见挑战：

  • 天气（我住的地方可能会下大雪！）
  • 活动（是否曾因强制性的建筑消防演习而中断办公室会议？）
  • 汽油（你最近看到燃料价格了吗？？）
  • 如果乘坐公共交通，与公共交通相关的问题（可能包括上述任何一项，以及以下独特的考虑因素，包括交通通行证故障、意外延误或线路关闭、人身安全和公共卫生问题等）

消除所有这些因素后，我已经有好几年没有上班迟到了。 早高峰的压力已经不复存在。

我曾经相信“工作/生活平衡”，并花费大量精力计划如何最好地分割一天中的时间，并付出相当大的努力将我的工作生活与我的“家庭”或“现实”生活明确分开。 最终，我学会了完善这种平衡，甚至利用通勤作为家庭和办公室之间的一种缓冲。 这些天来，我体验到了我所谓的“生活平衡”。

对我来说，生活平衡仅仅意味着能够围绕我的“家”生活过“真实”的生活。 不得不处理“家”的事情，比如孩子放学回家，或者在工作日被要求在场修理电器，这可能会导致或加剧雇主/雇员关系中的摩擦。 现在，当一个意想不到的“家”情况出现时，我只是简单地处理它，我通常只花几分钟照顾它。 说起…

家庭事务

我之前提到过，我已经用不同的日常通勤代替了我的工作日通勤。 如果您还没有从上面猜到，我每天都会带我的孩子上学。 当我的日程允许时，我也会去接他们。 这里需要注意的是，这些学校都相对较近，但我想说的是，我完全可以做到这一点。

我们大多数人可能在至少有一位父母或监护人在家外工作的情况下长大。 就我而言，我父亲的工作经常让他远离家乡。 当他在城里而不是在外地时，他在办公室工作了很长时间。 我很痛苦地承认，在我成长的岁月里，这段经历给我们的关系带来了压力，但我们一直在努力弥补，我很高兴地说我们现在处于一个好地方。

我不希望我的孩子有同样的经历。 我本来希望我爸爸多在家，但他无能为力。 作为父母，我们应该为我们的孩子想要的比我们拥有的更好，即使我们长大的东西比较好。 我想不出比出现在孩子们的日常生活中更好的方法来改善我所拥有的东西。 另外，当我试图通过高峰时段的交通时，不需要放学后的安排，也不必担心他们会在哪里或与谁在一起。

比起晚上和周末更经常见到我的妻子也很高兴。 我很确定她对我开始保持厨房清洁这一事实感到兴奋，因为我也开始在项目任务或会议之间清理头脑！

而且，最重要的是，我认为我们的金毛寻回犬从未如此快乐过——无限制的腹部按摩！

包起来

我意识到许多工作不能无限期地在家完成，甚至根本无法完成！ 我不是在这里争辩说我们都应该回到封锁期间的工作方式。 如果您从事此类工作并希望主要在办公室工作，请务必这样做！ 我要说的是，没有一种万能的工作方法，尤其是在科技领域。

在家工作让我有机会简单地过自己的生活，极大地改善了我的生活。 因为我喜欢项目符号列表：

  • 我没有被束缚在一张不舒服的桌子上，也没有被锁在每天的通勤路上
  • 我不会错过与妻子和孩子共度一生的宝贵时刻
  • 我并不执着于将我的时间分配在两个看似独立的生活中，因此我更加专注于工作
  • 我可以处理日常起起落落的生活需求，对我的客户或项目绝对没有影响或中断
  • 还有许多直接与工作相关的好处，但我可能会在另一篇文章中讨论它们，因为它们超出了本文的范围。 底线：

    我从来没有像现在在 Artisan 全职在家工作更快乐。 我全身心地投入到我的项目中，感觉自己是我所在团队的正式成员。 我不必不断地争取工作/生活的平衡感，因为我可以简单地生活和工作。 我可以养家糊口，不会错过与他们在一起的时间。 归根结底，这就是我所追求的。

    远程工作将如何使您的生活受益？



原文:

This also appears on the Artisan Insights blog.

At the time of this writing I am approaching my first anniversary of working from home for a remote company. Since WFH is in the news so much these days, I was invited to candidly share my personal perspective of the remote work lifestyle and the primary ways this arrangement has benefited my life as a knowledge worker. What follows are my own experience and opinions.

I'm in my mid-30s and have spent the majority of my IT career based from a cubicle in an office building. I am now a technical consultant working on various client projects from home. I wasn't sure what to expect at first, but after two and a half years based from my home office I've come to fully appreciate the benefits of the remote working lifestyle.

Let's start with the obvious first:

Commute "Sch-Moot"

Actually, I do still have a "daily commute" –sort of. What I've done is replace commuting to work with a different daily routine I'll talk about later. The difference now is that the common challenges of a typical commute have been eliminated for me:

  • Weather (It can snow fairly heavily where I live!)
  • Events (Ever been interrupted from an in-office meeting by a mandatory building fire drill?)
  • Gas (Have you seen fuel prices lately??)
  • If taking public transit, public transit-related issues (Which can include any of the above, plus the following unique considerations including transit pass glitches, unexpected delays or line closures, personal safety and public health concerns, etc.)

With all of these factors eliminated, it's been years since I've been late to work. The stress of the morning rush simply no longer exists.

"Life Balance"

I used to believe in "work/life balance" and spent considerable mental energy planning how best to segment my time throughout the day, putting considerable effort into clearly separating my work life from my "home" or "real" life. Eventually I learned to perfect that balance, even utilizing commutes as a sort of buffer between home and the office. These days, I experience what I call "life balance.”

For me, life balance simply means the ability to live my "real" life around my "home" life. Having to deal with "home" matters like children being home from school sick or being required to be present for an appliance repair during the workday used to have the potential to cause or exacerbate friction in the employer/employee relationship. Now, when an unexpected “home” situation manifests, I simply take care of it and I usually only end up spending a few minutes tending to it. Speaking of…

Family Matters

I mentioned earlier that I've replaced my workday commute with a different routine. If you haven't already guessed from the above, I take my kids to school every day. When my schedule allows, I will also pick them up. The caveat here may be that the schools are all relatively close, but the actual point I'm trying to make is that I'm able to do this at all.

Most of us likely grew up with at least one parent or guardian working outside of the home. In my case, my dad's jobs took him far away from home frequently. He worked long hours at the office when he was in town and not in the field. It pains me to admit that the experience put a strain on our relationship during my formative years but we have been working toward making amends and I am very happy to say that we are now in a good place.

I don't want this same experience for my kids. I would have wanted for my dad to be home more but he couldn't help that. As parents, we should want better for our children than what we had, even if what we had growing up was relatively good. I can't think of a better way to improve upon what I had than to be present in the daily lives of my children. Plus, no need for after school arrangements and no worrying about where or who they'll be with while I try to fight my way through rush hour traffic.

It's also nice seeing my wife more often than evenings and weekends. I'm pretty sure she's thrilled by the fact that I've taken up keeping the kitchen clean as a way to clear my head between project tasks or meetings, too!

And, most importantly, I don't think our Golden Retriever has ever been happier — unlimited belly rubs!

Wrapping Up

I realize that many jobs cannot be done from home indefinitely, or even at all whatsoever! I'm not here to argue that we should all go back to working how we had to during lockdown. If you do this kind of work and want to work mostly in an office, please do– by all means! What I am saying is there is no one-size-fits-all approach to work, especially in tech.

Working from home has greatly improved my life by allowing me the opportunity to simply live my life. Since I love bulleted lists:

  • I'm not chained to an uncomfortable desk or locked into a grinding daily commute
  • I'm not missing valuable, once-in-a-lifetime moments with my wife and children
  • I'm not obsessing over dividing my time between two seemingly separate lives and am therefore more present and engaged at work
  • I can handle the daily ebb and flow of life demands with absolutely no impact or interruption to my client or projects
  • There are many directly work-related benefits as well, but I will perhaps discuss them in a different post as they fall outside of the scope of this article. (Part Two, anyone?)

Bottom line:

I’ve never been happier than I am now working from home full-time at Artisan. I'm fully engaged in my projects and feel like a full member of the teams I'm part of. I don't have to constantly strive for a sense of work/life balance as I can simply live and work. I can provide for my family and not miss time with them. At the end of the day, that's all I'm after.

How would working remotely benefit your life?
item.author: twop0intfive

item.title: 给美国的消息：请去投票！ | Message to those in the United States: Please go vote!
item.description:
我真的不想在这里陷入政治话题。 但我只想简单地敦促有资格投票的美国公民确保您已注册并请在 11 月 8 日的中期选举中投票。

有这么多消息四处流传，政治广告足以在情感上和精神上打败你。 但对我的同胞们，我说：“在这篇文章中，美国仍然是一个代议制民主国家。请行使你的公民义务并投票！”

尝试至少对您关心的问题有一个基本的了解。 请至少粗略查看一下候选人的投票历史，看看他们的行为是否符合他们的言辞。

您可能认为您的投票并不重要，但它确实如此。

恳求版主

我希望我没有在这个网站上冒犯任何人。 但这很重要。 如果这篇文章违反了网站指南，我会理解删除它。

原文:

I really don't want to get bogged down in a political thread here. But I do want to simply urge US citizens who are eligible to vote, to make sure you're registered and please go vote in the November 8 midterm election.

There are so many messages floating around and the political ads are enough to emotionally and mentally beat you up pretty good. But to my fellow citizens, I say: "The United States is still a representative democracy as of this post. Please exercise your civic duty and vote!"

Try have at least a basic understanding of issues that matter to you. Please do, at least, a cursory look into voting histories of your candidates to see if their actions match their rhetoric.

You may not think your vote matters, but it does.

Plea to Moderators

I hope I have not offended anyone on this site. But this is important. I will understand the removal of this post if it violates site guidelines.

item.title: 我们如何通过有意的反馈促进开放对话 | How We Foster Open Dialogue Through Intentional Feedback
item.description:
还是您发现自己很放松，甚至对新机会的期待感到兴奋？ 员工对这个问题的回答可以很好地说明他们所在公司的文化。 这也可以很好地表明他们是否拥有更开放或更封闭的工作环境。

在 The Gnar，我们营造一种开放的工作文化，让员工在给予和接受反馈、分享想法和寻找成长机会时感到自在。

我们如何做到这一点？ 首先，通过创建支持和鼓励公开对话的格式。

反馈格式

年度审核

员工与联合创始人尼克和迈克以随意的对话形式会面，讨论他们一直在从事的项目、他们想要改进的领域以及最近令他们兴奋的事情。

每月 1:1s

每月 1 对 1 会议由我们的运营主管 Heather 主持，他为签到、分享反馈和在更个人的层面上聊天创造了一个安全的环境。

在工作 90 天后，新员工的队友被要求完成一项关于他们绩效的匿名调查，然后在下一个月的 1:1 中与新员工分享和审查。

每日反馈

作为我们代码审查流程的一部分，员工会就彼此的工作提供和接收详细的反馈。 这是我们利用协作每天改进代码的关键方式。

每周团队回顾

回顾会议每周四在午餐时间举行。 我们聚集在一个大小组中讨论整个公司，并轮流提出改进流程的方法。

复古项目

参与结束后，项目团队会聚在一起审查整个项目并讨论潜在的流程改进。 这可确保所有团队成员都有机会做出贡献并感到被倾听。

过程回顾

我们会定期审查具体流程以评估有效性并梳理出任何潜在的改进。 例如，我们最近重新审视了公司范围内的日常​​签到的格式，以确保它们仍然与不断扩大的团队合作。

建议牛

虽然我们的团队倾向于公开分享，但如果有任何时间或情况让您感到不舒服，提供此选项非常重要。

反馈文化

定调

创建分享反馈的容器是第一步，但更重要的是培养一种让给予和接受反馈变得舒适的文化。 在核心，纳尔的非等级结构和“低自我”精神有助于创造这种动态。 最重要的是，我们每天都在实践的代码审查流程鼓励采用协作、非教条式、头脑风暴式的方法来提供和接收反馈。

在我们的团队回顾中，每个团队成员都有机会分享进展顺利的地方、可以改进的地方，以及我们如何调整流程以进行这些改进。 作为一项规则，我们会完全倾听对方的意见，并且永远不会用争论来回应别人的观点。 这鼓励人们更真实、更频繁地分享，确保每个人都能安全地分享他们的全部意见而无需评判。

即使在年度评论（The Gnar 最正式的反馈格式）中，Mike 和 Nick 也利用这段时间作为指导机会，庆祝成就并根据自己的经验提供针对挑战的具体指导。

他以身作则。”
跟进

如果鼓励员工分享改进建议，重要的是他们看到这些改进正在实际实施。 毕竟，我们的目标不仅仅是听取对方的意见，而是让我们的组织变得更好。

在每个团队回顾结束时，我们会审查具体的行动项目并将其分配给适当的团队成员来实施。 尼克和迈克对尝试新事物持开放态度，并且会给大多数建议一试，至少作为试验看看它是如何进行的。 因此，Team Retros 产生了许多变化，包括公司政策的变化。

这里有一些例子：

  • 假期和育儿假的调整
  • 定期活动的实施，例如项目演示、咖啡时间、欢乐时光和“展示 Codez”，这是我们展示我们兴奋的代码的每月聚会
  • 安排许多关于技术主题的棕色包演示
  • 聘请演讲嘉宾
  • 代码审查策略
  • 更新新的候选人模拟和面试流程

塑造未来

我们一直回到的一个想法是，文化不是由上层决定的，而是每个员工独特贡献的结果。 当我们招聘时，我们寻找的是文化的补充，而不是文化的契合。

分享、吸收和根据反馈做出改变是我们在 The Gnar 将我们的文化带入生活的关键方式之一。 新的流程、方法和政策是建议和实施它们的人的直接结果。 因此，正是员工自己塑造了 The Gnar 的未来。

详细了解The Gnar 和我们的团队。



原文:

Does the phrase “annual review” make you tense up? Or do you find yourself relaxed, maybe even excited with the anticipation of new opportunities? An employee’s answer to this question can say a lot about the culture of the company they're working for. It can also be a pretty good indicator of whether they have a more open or closed work environment.

At The Gnar, we foster an open work culture, where employees feel comfortable giving and receiving feedback, sharing ideas, and looking for opportunities to grow.

How do we do this? First, by creating formats that support and encourage open dialogue.

Feedback Formats

Annual Reviews

Employees meet with co-founders Nick and Mike in a casual, conversational format to talk through projects they’ve been working on, areas they want to improve, and what’s been exciting them lately.

Monthly 1:1s

Monthly 1:1 meetings are led by our Operations Lead, Heather, who creates a safe environment for checking in, sharing feedback, and chatting on a more personal level.

90-Day Feedback

After 90 days on the job, a new hire’s teammates are asked to complete an anonymous survey on their performance, which is then shared and reviewed with the new hire in their next monthly 1:1.

Daily Feedback

As part of our code review process, employees give and receive detailed feedback on each other’s work. This is a key way we leverage collaboration to improve our code on a daily basis.

Weekly Team Retros

Retrospective meetings take place every Thursday over lunch. We gather in a large group to discuss the company as a whole and take turns suggesting ways to improve processes.

Project Retros

After an engagement has ended, the project team gets together to review the project as a whole and discuss potential process improvements. This ensures all team members have a chance to contribute and feel heard.

Process Retros

Periodically, we review specific processes to gauge effectiveness and tease out any potential improvements. For example, we recently revisited the format of our daily company-wide check-ins to ensure they still work with an expanding group.

Suggestion Ox

Suggestion Ox is a simple tool an employee can use to submit feedback anonymously. While our team tends to share publicly, it’s important to have this option available should there ever be a time or situation that feels uncomfortable.

Feedback Culture

Setting the Tone

Creating the container to share feedback is step one, but even more crucial is fostering a culture that makes giving and receiving feedback comfortable. At the core, The Gnar’s non-hierarchical structure and “low ego” ethos lends well to creating this dynamic. On top of that, our code review process, which we practice daily, encourages a collaborative, non-dogmatic, brainstorming-style approach to giving and receiving feedback.

In our Team Retros, each team member has a chance to share what has been going well, what could use improvement, and how we might adjust processes to make those improvements. As a rule, we hear each other out completely and never respond to another person’s point with an argument. This encourages people to share more authentically and more often by ensuring everyone feels safe to share their full opinion without judgment.

Even in Annual Reviews (the most formal of The Gnar’s feedback formats) Mike and Nick use the time as a coaching opportunity, celebrating accomplishments and providing specific guidance on challenges based on their own experience.

“Mike helps guide me, he doesn’t just tell me what to do. He leads by example.”
-Taylor Kearns, software developer

Following Up

If employees are being encouraged to share suggestions for improvement, it's important they see those improvements actually being implemented. The goal, after all, is more than simply hearing each other out, but making our organization better as a result.

At the end of each Team Retro, we review specific action items and assign them to the appropriate team members to implement. Nick and Mike are open to trying new things and will give most suggestions a shot, at least as a trial to see how it goes. As a result, many changes have come out of Team Retros, including company policy changes.

Here are a few examples:

  • Adjustments to holidays and parental leave
  • Implementation of regular events, such as project demos, coffee hour, happy hour, and “Show the Codez,” a monthly meetup where we showcase code we’re excited about
  • Scheduling numerous brown bag presentations on technical topics
  • Hiring guest speakers
  • Code review strategies
  • Updates to the new candidate simulation and interview process

Shaping the Future

An idea we come back to all the time is that culture is not dictated from the top, but formed as a result of every employee’s unique contribution. When we hire, we search for culture adds, not culture fits.

Sharing, absorbing, and making changes based on feedback is one of the key ways we bring our culture to life here at The Gnar. New processes, methodologies, and policies are a direct result of the people who suggest and implement them. As a result, it is the employees themselves who shape the future of The Gnar.

Learn more about The Gnar and our team.
item.author: efertsch

item.title: 解释数组方法 | Explaining Array Methods
item.description:
在学习 javascript 时，处理 javascript 的基础知识是一个非常重要的方面。 在本文中，我将重点介绍如何处理 javascript 的基础知识，并将重点放在数组方法上。

数组方法 Map、Sort、Reduce 和 filter 在完全理解后将真正提高您的 javascript 技能，我将抛出一些关于如何更好地使用这些方法的示例。

一些程序员确实将这种方法称为函数式编程的入门药物

我将使用下面的数据来更好地解释这些方法。

常量发明者 = [

在上面，每个发明者都是一个对象

所以让我们开始吧！

过滤器的工作原理是向它传递一个函数，该函数将遍历数组中从第一个对象到最后一个对象的每个项目，然后给出一个新的值数组，该数组对所请求的内容返回 true（条件语句或诸如此类的东西！）

下面给出一个例子

过滤器（功能（发明者）

返回真；

控制台.table（十六）；

我喜欢把 Map 想象成一台工厂机器，它接收原材料，对其进行提炼，然后将其从另一侧取出。 与过滤器不同的是，它过滤并仅显示需要的内容，地图会在您提供项目时将其显示出来。

下面给出一个例子

常量全名 = 发明者.Map(发明者 => 发明者.first + ' ' + invnetor.last);

排序通过获取两个项目来工作，一个例子是当您被要求仅对这两个项目进行排序时，例如，如果人 A 比人 B 年长，那么将人 A 放在上面，然后 B 跟随。

这是通过返回 1 然后返回 -1 来完成的，这会在数组中上下冒泡项目。

下面给出一个例子

常量顺序 = 发明者。排序（函数（a，b）{

如果 (a.year > b.year){ 返回 1;

返回-1；

控制台.table（订单）；

它允许您在每一个（对象）上构建一些东西（对象），与其他数组方法不同，它需要多个操作

下面给出一个例子；

返回总计 + (inventor.passed —inventor.year);

上面的解释并不是对 Array 方法及其在 javascript 中的使用的简明透彻的解释，本文仅对 Array 方法及其定义和示例进行简要说明

谢谢阅读！



原文:

Dealing with the fundamentals of javascript is a very important aspect when learning javascript. In this article, I will be writing on how to deal with the fundamentals of javascript with my focus on Array Methods.

The array methods Map, Sort, Reduce and filter will really improve your javascript skills when understood perfectly, I will be throwing out some bunch of examples on how to get better at working with these methods.

Some programmers do refer to this methods as the gateway drugs to functional programming

I will be working with the Data below to explain the methods better.

Const inventors = [

{ first: ‘mark’, last: ‘zuck’, year: 1600, passed: 1879 },

{ first: ‘2pac’, last: ‘Shakur’, year: 1660, passed: 1779 },

{ first: ‘Armando’, last: ‘Maradona’, year: 1653, passed: 1856 },

{ first: ‘Eminem’, last: ‘marshal’, year: 1578, passed: 1688 },

{ first: ‘Pele’, last: ‘Nascimento’, year: 1509, passed: 1859 },

{ first: ‘Michael’, last: ‘Jordan’, year: 1590, passed: 1679 },

{ first: ‘Elon’, last: ‘reeve’, year: 1630, passed: 1899 }

];

In the above, each inventor is an object

So let’s kick it off!

** .Filter**
Filter works by passing in it a function that will loop over every single item in an array from the first object to the last which then gives a new array of the values that returns true to what was requested for ( a conditional statement or whatnot! )

An example is given below

// Array.prototype.filter()

// filter the list of inventors for those born in 1600

Const Sixteen = inventors. Filter(function(inventor)

{ If (inventor.year >= 1600 && <=1699) {

Return True;

} };

console.table(sixteen);

** .Map**

Map takes in an Array, does a particular instruction with the array and then returns a new array but of the same length

I like to think of Map as a factory machine that takes in a raw material, Refines it and then bring it out on the other side. Unlike filter, where it filters and bring out only what is needed, Map will bring out the item as you give it.

An example is given below

//Array.prototype.map

//give us an array of the inventory first and last names

Const fullnames = inventors.Map(inventor => inventor.first + ‘ ‘ + invnetor.last);

Console.log(fullnames);

** .Sort **

Sort works by taking two items, an instance is when you are asked to sort just those two items e.g. if person A is older than person B if so put person A on top and then B follows.

This is done by returning 1 and then -1 which bubble the items up and down in the array.

An example is given below

//Array.prototype.sort

//sort the inventors by birthdate, oldest to youngest

Const order = inventors.Sort(function(a, b) {

If (a.year > b.year ){ Return 1;

} else {

Return -1;

} });

console.table(order);

PS: you can also use ternary operator for the above.

** .Reduce **

Reduce works like a SORT in a MAP, it takes items in and then sort them to something else or filter them down.

It allows you to build something(object) on every single one(object), it takes in multiple actions unlike the other array methods

An example is given below;

//Array.prototype.reduce

//How many years did all the inventors live ?

Const totalYears = inventors.reduce((total, inventor) =>{

Return total + (inventor.passed — inventor.year);

}, 0);

console.log(totalYears);

The above explained is not a concise and thorough explanation of Array methods and their usage in javascript, this article only gives a brief explanation about the Array methods, their definitions and examples

Thanks for Reading!
